original_hypothesis.title: Minting Tokens to Blacklisted Addresses Bypasses Compliance Controls
original_hypothesis.hypothesis_description: mint() does not call adminSanity() and does not check isBlacklisted() for the recipient address. A minter can mint tokens directly to sanctioned/blacklisted addresses, bypassing the blacklist compliance mechanism. burn() NatSpec claims 'Validates that sender is not blacklisted' but performs no such check.
original_hypothesis.investigation_rationale: For a regulated EUR stablecoin, compliance with sanctions is critical. The ability to mint tokens to blacklisted addresses creates regulatory risk and contradicts the stated compliance model. The burn() NatSpec discrepancy suggests the developer intended these checks but failed to implement them.

proof_of_signal.pattern: mint() has no isBlacklisted(to) check; burn() NatSpec at line 152 says 'Validates that sender is not blacklisted' but code has no blacklist check

preconditions: Address X is blacklisted via blacklist(X)
MINTER_ROLE or MASTER_MINTER calls mint(X, amount)

initial_economic_evidence.manipulation_vector: Minter creates tokens for sanctioned entity, bypassing compliance
initial_economic_evidence.profit_hypothesis: Sanctioned entity receives tokens, potentially laundering value through subsequent transfers after unblacklisting

economic_questions: Can a blacklisted address that received minted tokens transfer them?
Does this create regulatory liability for the protocol?
Is burn() also missing blacklist checks as NatSpec suggests?
validation_requirements: Test: blacklist(addr) then mint(addr, amount) - confirm success
Test: blacklist(minter) then minter calls burn() - confirm success
Verify NatSpec discrepancy in burn() implementation

preliminary_attack_economics.potential_profit_range: Regulatory/reputational damage rather than direct profit

market_conditions_analysis.market_impact_assessment: Regulatory shutdown risk if compliance violation discovered

potential_victim_impact.direct_victims: Protocol and all token holders (regulatory risk)
potential_victim_impact.total_value_at_risk: Full protocol value at regulatory risk

investigation_priorities.calculation_validation: Confirm blacklist has no effect on mint/burn

validation_result.impact_summary: Filtered before validation: attack_realism is LOW
validation_result.detailed_analysis: This hypothesis was auto-rejected because its attack_realism grade is LOW. Preconditions require actions outside the attacker's control (off-chain compromise, victim cooperation, or admin collusion). No LLM validation was performed.
